idea-plugin.name: AI Assistant Provocations
idea-plugin.description:
Most AI coding tools optimize for speed. Tool for Thought optimizes for understanding.
Instead of generating code on demand and getting out of the way, it stays present throughout your workflow. When you finish a method, it reads what you wrote and surfaces 1–2 targeted provocations: edge cases you might have missed, structural decisions worth questioning, gaps between what the code does and what it probably should. When you start a new method, it gently asks what you're trying to achieve – not to slow you down, but to make any assistance you get more meaningful.
Everything happens inline. Provocations appear as unobtrusive inlay hints at the closing brace. Ghost text prompts intent before you write a single line of a method body. A thought log captures everything you dismissed or didn't get to. Nothing interrupts your flow. Nothing is mandatory.
The goal isn't to make you dependent on another tool. It's the opposite: to keep your critical thinking engaged even as AI takes on more of the mechanical work.
